clusters.failureShape: The page has no "skip to main content" link (or other bypass mechanism), so keyboard and screen-reader users must tab through the entire navigation/header on every page to reach the main content.
clusters.fix: Add a "Skip to main content" link as the first focusable element on the page, targeting the id of the main content region. Make it visible on focus.

clusters.failureShape: A "skip to main content" link exists but doesn't move focus when activated — focus stays on the link — so the bypass doesn't actually work.
clusters.fix: Point the skip link's href at the id of the main content container and give that target tabindex="-1" so the browser moves focus into it on activation.

clusters.failureShape: The skip link is present but not the first focusable element — it's preceded by cookie-banner buttons or a third-party tool — so users tab through other controls before reaching the bypass.
clusters.fix: Place the skip link first in the DOM/tab order, ahead of cookie banners and injected widgets, so it's the very first thing a keyboard user reaches.
